title: Heat Exhaustion
immediate_steps: Move to a cooler, shaded, or air-conditioned environment
Lie down and elevate legs slightly
Loosen or remove heavy clothing
Sip cool water or a sports drink
Apply cool, wet cloths to the skin or take a cool bath
If symptoms worsen or last longer than an hour, seek medical attention
avoid: Do not drink ice-cold water too quickly (can cause stomach cramps)
Do not drink alcohol or caffeine
Do not ignore symptoms as it can progress to heat stroke

title: Heat Stroke
symptoms: Hot, red, dry, or damp skin (no sweating)
immediate_steps: Call emergency services immediately (this is a medical emergency)
Move the person to a cool environment
Rapidly cool the person by immersing them in a cold bath or shower if possible
Alternatively, sponge with cold water and fan vigorously
Place ice packs on the neck, armpits, and groin
Monitor breathing and be prepared for CPR
avoid: Do not give the person fluids to drink if they have altered mental status or are vomiting
Do not give fever-reducing medications like acetaminophen or ibuprofen (they are harmful in heat stroke)
Do not leave the person alone

title: Heat Stroke - Recognition and Immediate Action
immediate_steps: Immediately move the person to a cooler or shaded area
Remove excess clothing to help cool the body
Begin cooling measures such as applying cool water to the skin, using wet cloths, or fanning the person
Call emergency services immediately

title: Heat Stroke - Symptoms and Risk Factors
immediate_steps: Identify risk factors: dehydration, direct sun exposure, lack of ventilation, strenuous outdoor activity, wearing heavy/dark clothing

title: Heat Stroke - Recovery and Medical Care
immediate_steps: Immediate medical attention is necessary, even if the person improves after initial cooling
Continue monitoring body temperature, breathing, and consciousness until help arrives
Keep the person lying down in a cool environment
If the person becomes unconscious, place them in a recovery position to maintain airway safety
avoid: Do not give any medications unless prescribed by medical professionals
